annotations.label: Affine geometry

anchor.snippet: affine geometry is what remains of Euclidean geometry when ignoring

annotations.note: Mathlib develops affine geometry via the `AffineSpace` notation (an `AddTorsor`) but has no encompassing 'affine geometry' object.

annotations.label: Playfair's axiom

anchor.snippet: Playfair's axiom (Given a line L and a point P not on L

annotations.note: No formalization of Playfair's axiom was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine transformations

anchor.snippet: Comparisons of figures in affine geometry are made with affine transformations

annotations.note: `AffineMap k P1 P2` is defined as a function whose action commutes with the linear-map difference.

annotations.label: Synthetic affine space

anchor.snippet: In synthetic geometry , an affine space is a set of points to which is associated a set of lines

annotations.note: Mathlib's `AffineSpace` is via linear algebra (AddTorsor), not synthetic axioms on points and lines.

annotations.label: Affine space via linear algebra

anchor.snippet: Affine geometry can also be developed on the basis of linear algebra

annotations.note: `AffineSpace V P` is a scoped notation for `AddTorsor V P`, exactly the linear-algebra approach.

annotations.label: Point-vector operations on affine space

anchor.snippet: this amounts to having an operation that associates to any ordered pair of points a vector

annotations.note: `AddTorsor` axioms `vsub_vadd` and `vadd_vsub` capture the point-minus-point and vector-plus-point operations.

annotations.label: Pappus' affine parallel property

anchor.snippet: Suppose A, B, C are on one line and A', B', C' on another.

annotations.note: No Pappus-type theorem in an affine setting found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pappus axiom system

anchor.snippet: The full axiom system proposed has point , line , and line containing point as primitive notions

annotations.note: No synthetic axiomatic system with point/line/incidence primitives is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine axiom of parallelism

anchor.snippet: Given a point A and a line r not through A , there is at most one line through A which does not meet r .

annotations.note: Mathlib defines parallelism of affine subspaces, but does not state the synthetic at-most-one-parallel axiom.

annotations.label: Desargues axiom

anchor.snippet: Given seven distinct points A, A', B, B', C, C', O

annotations.note: No Desargues' theorem or axiom is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Parallelism is an equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: The affine concept of parallelism forms an equivalence relation on lines.

annotations.note: `Parallel.equivalence` shows parallelism is an `Equivalence` on `AffineSubspace k P`.

annotations.label: Minor affine Desargues property

anchor.snippet: A plane is said to have the "minor affine Desargues property" when two triangles in parallel perspective

annotations.note: No formalization of the minor affine Desargues property was found.

annotations.label: Vectors form an abelian group

anchor.snippet: the vectors form an abelian group under addition

annotations.note: Mathlib assumes the vector type is an `AddGroup` (or `AddCommGroup`) rather than deriving the group structure from a synthetic ternary-ring construction.

annotations.label: Affinities preserve collinearity

anchor.snippet: affine transformations (affinities) preserve collinearity

annotations.note: `Collinear` is defined and characterised, but a dedicated lemma that `AffineMap`/`AffineEquiv` preserves collinearity was not located.

annotations.label: Affine theorem

anchor.snippet: We identify as affine theorems any geometric result that is invariant under the affine group

annotations.note: This is a meta-mathematical notion; not formalized as a concept in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine group as semidirect product

anchor.snippet: The affine group is generated by the general linear group and the translations

annotations.note: No `AffineGroup` group or semidirect-product decomposition with GL is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Concurrence at the centroid

anchor.snippet: the theorem from the plane geometry of triangles about the concurrence of the lines joining each vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side

annotations.note: Mathlib defines simplex medians/centroid and proves the centroid lies on each median dividing it n:1, generalizing the triangle concurrence statement.

annotations.label: Envelope of area-bisecting lines

anchor.snippet: the lines that divide the area of a triangle into two equal halves form an envelope inside the triangle

annotations.note: No envelope-of-area-bisecting-lines result for triangles in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine-invariant area and volume formulas

anchor.snippet: Familiar formulas such as half the base times the height for the area of a triangle

annotations.note: Mathlib has measure-theoretic volume but not the elementary half-base-times-height triangle area as an affine-invariant statement.

annotations.label: Galilean kinematics via shear mapping

anchor.snippet: The shear mapping of a plane with an axis for each represents coordinate change for an observer moving with velocity v in a resting frame of reference

annotations.note: No Galilean change-of-frame via shear mapping in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Relativistic kinematics via squeeze mapping

anchor.snippet: The method involves rapidity instead of velocity, and substitutes squeeze mapping for the shear mapping used earlier.

annotations.note: No relativistic squeeze-mapping/rapidity treatment in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine space (coordinatized)

anchor.snippet: Affine geometry can be viewed as the geometry of an affine space of a given dimension n , coordinatized over a field K .

annotations.note: `AffineSpace V P` with `V` a module over a ring/field captures the coordinatized affine space.

annotations.label: Affine space as restricted linear combinations

anchor.snippet: Affine space can also be viewed as a vector space whose operations are limited to those linear combinations whose coefficients sum to one

annotations.note: `affineCombination` together with the sum-of-weights-equal-one hypotheses (e.g. `weightedVSubOfPoint_vadd_eq_of_sum_eq_one`) realises this viewpoint.

annotations.label: Affine planes

anchor.snippet: affine planes are 2-dimensional affine geometries defined in terms of the relations between points and lines

annotations.note: No synthetic incidence-geometry `AffinePlane` axiomatisation is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine transformation (projective view)

anchor.snippet: an affine transformation is a projective transformation that does not permute finite points with points at infinity

annotations.note: Mathlib does not characterise affine maps as projective transformations fixing the hyperplane at infinity.
